name: video2x
description: A machine learning-based video super resolution and frame interpolation framework. Est. Hack the Valley II, 2018.
readme_summary: Video2X is a C++ framework for enhancing video quality through machine learning. It offers super-resolution to upscale video resolution and frame interpolation to increase frame rates, utilizing advanced AI models and GPU acceleration.
key_features: Machine learning-based video super resolution
Machine learning-based frame interpolation
Rewrite in C/C++ for speed and efficiency
Cross-platform support (Windows, Linux)
Support for Anime4K v4, Real-ESRGAN, Real-CUGAN, RIFE
GPU acceleration via Vulkan and ncnn
New GUI and installer for Windows
primary_use_case: Upscaling low-resolution videos and increasing the frame rate of existing videos using AI for improved visual quality.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist in a futuristic laboratory, meticulously transforming a blurry, pixelated video reel into a crystal-clear, high-definition stream. The alchemist uses glowing tools that project intricate neural network patterns onto the input reel, which then unfurls into a sharp, vibrant output. Small holographic screens display AI model names like 'Anime4K' and 'Real-ESRGAN', and a subtle 'Video2X' logo hovers near the alchemist's workstation. The scene is bathed in a cool, technological glow, with data particles flowing between the input and output. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a focus on the magical yet precise transformation process.

name: CloudPaste
description: 🌩️ Serverless 自托管的文件管理与文本分享工具/网盘，支持多存储聚合管理和WebDAV 服务挂载。兼容 S3、WebDAV、OneDrive、Google Drive、Telegram、HF、GitHub 等。内置   Markdown 编辑器、30+文件格式预览。支持 Cloudflare Workers 或 Docker 部署。
readme_summary: CloudPaste is a serverless, self-hosted file management and text-sharing tool that aggregates multiple storage services. It offers a built-in Markdown editor, supports over 30 file format previews, and can be deployed via Cloudflare Workers or Docker.
key_features: Serverless architecture with Cloudflare Workers, Workflow, and D1
Support for multiple storage backends including S3-compatible, WebDAV, OneDrive, Google Drive, Telegram, HuggingFace, and GitHub
Built-in Markdown editor with real-time preview and export options
Direct preview for over 30 file formats
Flexible deployment options: Cloudflare Workers or Docker
Advanced upload features: pre-signed direct upload, streaming, chunked resumable uploads
Scheduled tasks for maintenance and synchronization
Unified visual management for multiple storage sources
primary_use_case: To provide a flexible, self-hosted, and serverless platform for managing and sharing files and text across various cloud storage providers, with integrated editing and preview capabilities.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized illustration of a central, glowing cloud hub acting as a universal adapter. From this hub, streams of digital data flow outwards, connecting to diverse icons representing different cloud storage services like S3, Google Drive, OneDrive, and Telegram. Small, elegant windows float around the hub, showcasing file previews and Markdown text being edited. A subtle, stylized 'CloudPaste' logo is integrated into the hub's design. The overall mood is efficient and interconnected, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be 3D isometric illustration with bright, optimistic colors and clear, defined shapes, emphasizing seamless integration and accessibility.

name: logo-generator-skill
readme_summary: This repository provides a professional SVG logo generator that creates multiple design variants based on product characteristics. It also generates high-end showcase images with various professional background styles, leveraging Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview for refined presentations.
key_features: SVG Logo Generation (dot matrix, line systems, mixed compositions)
Design Variety (6+ distinct variants per request)
Professional Showcase (12 curated background styles)
Nano Banana Integration (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview)
Interactive HTML Previews
Export as SVG and PNG
primary_use_case: Quickly generating professional, high-quality logos and presentation-ready showcase images for startups, developers, designers, and product teams.
cover_image_prompt: A master artisan, depicted as a stylized, glowing digital sculptor, meticulously shaping raw geometric forms (representing product characteristics) into a diverse array of elegant, minimalist logos. These logos are then seamlessly placed onto professional-looking display stands, each showcasing a different sophisticated background (like a spotlight, a fluid gradient, or a clean studio setting). Subtle UI elements and flowing data streams around the sculptor hint at the AI-driven generation process. The overall scene should convey creativity, professionalism, and efficiency, rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a polished, high-fidelity finish.

name: mxFind
description: Rust CLI/TUI tool for discovering public Matrix rooms through homeserver public room directories.
readme_summary: mxfind is a Rust-based command-line and terminal UI tool designed to discover public Matrix chat rooms. It allows users to query multiple Matrix homeservers, build a local SQLite index for fast searching, and output results in both human-readable and machine-readable JSON formats.
key_features: Search public Matrix rooms via `/_matrix/client/v3/publicRooms`
Query multiple configured homeservers
Store room metadata in a local SQLite index
Use local search by default when the database exists
Force live search when needed
Print compact human-readable search results
Print full JSON without truncating data
Inspect a single indexed room by room ID or canonical alias
Check homeserver availability with `mxfind status`
Show live server status next to rooms in human-readable output
Explore rooms through an experimental TUI with live search by default
Async networking with Tokio and Reqwest
primary_use_case: Discovering and searching public Matrix chat rooms across different homeservers.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, antique library filled with countless glowing books, each representing a public Matrix room. A curious explorer, holding a magnifying glass that displays a small, clean UI element, is sifting through these books. Some books are neatly arranged on shelves (representing the local SQLite index), while others are floating in the air, being actively examined (representing live search). Small, subtle digital particles emanate from the magnifying glass and the floating books, hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is one of discovery and organization in a vast digital space. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and clear, readable iconography.

name: CrosshairJs
description: Lightweight animated custom cursor with smart hover effects and zero setup.
readme_summary: Crosshair.JS is a lightweight JavaScript library that replaces the default browser cursor with a customizable, animated crosshair. It offers smart hover effects that make the cursor magnetically snap and scale to interactive elements like links and buttons, enhancing user experience with smooth animations and visual feedback.
key_features: Zero Markup - automatic style injection and DOM element handling
Magnetic Hover - snaps and scales to links and buttons
Customizable - control over colors, sizes, and blend modes
Performance - smooth 60fps animation using RequestAnimationFrame
MutationObserver - works with dynamic content and SPAs
primary_use_case: To enhance website user experience by replacing the standard mouse cursor with an engaging, animated, and interactive custom cursor that provides visual feedback on hover.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical wand, subtly shaped like a stylized crosshair, floats above a digital landscape representing a website. As the wand hovers over glowing icons of links and buttons, it emits a gentle magnetic pull, causing them to momentarily glow and scale towards the wand. Small, ethereal data particles stream from the wand, forming the outline of the custom cursor. The background features subtle code snippets and UI elements, with the name 'Crosshair.JS' elegantly integrated into the scene. The overall mood is playful and futuristic, rendered in a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration style with soft, glowing lighting.

name: Remote-PC-managment
description: System for remote PC managment without image streaming
readme_summary: This repository provides a full-featured system for remote monitoring and management of computers, featuring a Python/Flask server and a cross-platform Rust agent. It enables real-time command execution, file transfer, system monitoring, and administrative control through a web interface.
key_features: Agent management (registration, status monitoring, deletion)
Real-time shell/PowerShell command execution
File upload and download
System information gathering (OS, processes, services)
WebSocket for real-time communication
Web-based administrator panel
Operation auditing and logging
JWT authentication
Automatic TLS/SSL certificate generation
primary_use_case: Remote monitoring and management of computers within a local network or over the internet.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled digital puppeteer, with glowing threads of data extending from their fingertips, expertly manipulating a series of interconnected computer monitors arranged like marionettes on a stage. Each monitor displays a different system status or command output, controlled by the puppeteer's precise gestures. Subtle UI elements and code snippets are visible on small screens around the stage, hinting at the underlying technology. The scene is set in a dimly lit, futuristic control room, with a clean, focused composition and a cool, blue-toned color scheme. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing the control and management aspect.
